reasons.code_quality: The submitted application files are reasonably structured FastAPI code, but they are entirely irrelevant to the task — the target file (requirements.lock) was never touched.
reasons.completeness: The sole deliverable — updating the cryptography version in requirements.lock — is completely absent; the file is not present in the submission at all.
reasons.edge_cases: No edge cases are relevant or handled because the actual task (a one-line lock file edit) was not performed.
reasons.security: The security patch (bumping cryptography) was never applied, and the submitted settings.py contains a hardcoded JWT secret string, compounding the security posture failure.
reasons.plan_adherence: The submission bears no relation to the task plan — it submits application source files (database.py, routes, models, tests) instead of editing the single specified lock file.
reasons.scope_creep: The entire application codebase (16 files across app/, tests/, pyproject.toml, README.md) was submitted when only a single line in requirements.lock should have changed — this is maximal scope creep.
reasons.over_engineering: The task required a one-line sed-style edit; instead a full FastAPI application skeleton was submitted, representing extreme over-engineering relative to the actual requirement.

overall_notes: This submission is a complete miss: the required file (requirements.lock) does not appear anywhere, and none of the submitted files address the stated task. The submitted code appears to be the baseline seed project context rather than any deliberate change. Every scored dimension suffers because the core deliverable — bumping cryptography in the lock file — was never attempted.
